newsSinceJun6: No new fundamental news since June 6. Context: the stock spiked ~25% on June 1 to ~407,500 KRW on delivery-robot expansion hype and foreign/institutional buying (after a KRX investment-warning designation notice in May), then fully unwound to ~290,000 KRW by June 8-9 (~-29% off the spike high, flat today at +0.17%). The move was flow-driven speculation and its reversal, not a thesis event; the humanoid actuator (Dynamixel) and outdoor delivery-robot commercialization story is intact.

sources.label: Aju Fortune - ROBOTIS +25% June 1 spike on foreign/institutional buying
CBC News - ROBOTIS intraday +17% on delivery-robot business expansion
Daum Finance disclosure - KRX investment-warning designation notice (May 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new information since yesterday's hold, which was already issued at the post-crash price; today is flat (+0.17%). The full unwind of the speculative June 1 spike (KRX-flagged, flow-driven) de-crowds the name slightly and restores some asymmetry, but the 'fairly valued after Korea robotics rerating' thesis stands, so rank 27 holds with a small positive drift.
verdicts.bull: Top-tier actuator (Dynamixel) purity with reported adoption by a leading Chinese humanoid maker, plus real outdoor delivery-robot operating-fleet growth; the -29% retrace from the June 1 high removes froth without any fundamental deterioration.
verdicts.neutral: YTD is back to +7.6% and the speculative round-trip mostly restored the prior setup; at ~$2.65B cap it remains a fairly valued, cleaner humanoid-actuator comp without a fresh catalyst since June 6.
verdicts.bear: The KRX investment-warning episode shows the register is dominated by hot retail/momentum flow, and Schaeffler's preferred-supplier actuator deal with Humanoid (May 13) is a reminder that large motion-tech incumbents can crowd merchant actuator suppliers out of Western humanoid platforms.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Confirmed humanoid actuator design wins (Chinese or Western OEMs) or nationwide scaling of the outdoor delivery-robot fleet showing up in revenue.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Renewed speculative volatility/KRX trading designations and actuator competition from in-house and incumbent suppliers (e.g., Schaeffler) compressing the merchant-actuator opportunity.
